_comment: OSEN-1.0 Expert Configuration V3 — Production-Ready MoE Expert Specialization Map
_description: Llama 4 Scout base has 16 generic MoE experts. OSEN-1.0 extends to 20 experts with 4 new domain-specific experts for OS automation superiority. V3 fixes semantic donor alignment, adds Phase 4 validation, and tunes routing for real-world computer operation.
_architecture: Each layer has 20 experts with top-1 routing. The router learns to dispatch tokens to the most relevant expert during fine-tuning.

_changelog.v2.0: SVD structural transform, routing bias, curriculum learning, contrastive/diversity losses
_changelog.v3.0: Fixed semantic donor alignment (config ↔ inject_experts mismatch), added Phase 4 validation, expert co-activation matrix, upgraded training schedule, increased dataset targets, improved routing temperature annealing

expert_0.name: os_action_planner
expert_0.description: Plans multi-step OS operations: click sequences, keyboard shortcuts, window management, app launches. Decomposes user intent into atomic GUI actions with proper ordering and dependency resolution.

expert_1.name: screen_understanding
expert_1.description: Interprets screenshots, UI layouts, element positions, text on screen, dialog boxes, error messages, loading states, and visual feedback. Bridges vision encoder output to actionable understanding.

expert_2.name: error_recovery
expert_2.description: Specializes in identifying error states (crash dialogs, permission denials, network failures, UAC prompts), diagnosing root causes, and generating recovery action sequences. Handles stuck detection and fallback strategies.

expert_3.name: web_navigation
expert_3.description: Expert in browser automation: URL navigation, form filling, tab management, cookie/popup handling, CAPTCHA detection (not bypass), login flows, search engine usage, and web content extraction.

expert_4.name: code_generation
expert_4.description: Generates code in Python, JavaScript, PowerShell, batch scripts. Handles IDE interaction, terminal commands, code review, debugging workflows, and build/test cycles.

expert_5.name: file_system
expert_5.description: Manages file creation, deletion, moving, renaming, searching, permission changes, compression, and directory navigation across Windows/Linux/macOS file systems.

expert_6.name: natural_language_understanding
expert_6.description: Parses user commands in natural language (Korean, English, mixed), resolves ambiguity, extracts task parameters, and maps informal descriptions to precise OS operations.

expert_7.name: safety_ethics
expert_7.domain: Safety guardrails and ethical computer use
expert_7.description: Enforces safety constraints: blocks malicious commands, prevents data destruction, ensures user consent, validates permissions, detects social engineering attempts, and maintains audit trails.

expert_8.name: data_analysis
expert_8.description: Handles spreadsheet operations, data cleaning, statistical analysis, chart generation, CSV/JSON processing, database queries, and report compilation.

expert_9.name: system_administration
expert_9.description: Manages system settings, service control, registry editing, environment variables, network configuration, driver management, hardware diagnostics, and automation scheduling.

expert_10.name: text_composition
expert_10.description: Writes documents, emails, reports, presentations. Handles rich text formatting, template usage, grammar checking, and content structuring across various text editors.

expert_11.name: visual_design
expert_11.domain: Graphics, UI design, and image editing
expert_11.description: Creates and edits images in Paint, PowerPoint, or design tools. Handles color theory, layout principles, typography, asset export, and pixel-level precision.

expert_12.name: memory_context
expert_12.domain: Working memory, context tracking, and state persistence
expert_12.description: Maintains conversation context, tracks multi-step task progress, remembers discovered UI element positions, manages spatial memory for screen elements, and provides episodic recall of past actions.

expert_13.name: planning_strategy
expert_13.description: Decomposes complex goals into executable subtask sequences, estimates time/steps, handles replanning when blocked, manages inter-task dependencies, and optimizes execution order.

expert_14.name: input_method
expert_14.domain: Keyboard, mouse, IME, and accessibility input handling
expert_14.description: Specializes in precise input handling: IME state management (Korean/English toggle), keyboard shortcut execution, mouse coordinate targeting, drag-and-drop, scroll, and accessibility input methods.

expert_15.name: app_specific
expert_15.description: Knows application-specific UI patterns and workflows for common apps: Chrome, VS Code, MS Office, Paint, Terminal, File Explorer, Settings, Task Manager, etc. Maps app-specific affordances to actions.

expert_specializations._new_experts_comment: V3: Semantic donor alignment fixed. Donors now match functionally related base experts instead of stride-4 pattern.

expert_16.name: visual_grounding
expert_16.domain: Pixel-precise UI element localization and interaction targeting
expert_16.description: Bridges the gap between vision understanding and action execution. Given a screenshot and an intent ('click the Save button'), precisely identifies the target element's bounding box coordinates, handles overlapping elements, distinguishes between similar-looking buttons, and computes optimal click coordinates accounting for element state (enabled/disabled/hidden). This is the critical expert that makes osen-1.0 superior to generic models in actual computer control.
expert_16.init_strategy: V3: Blend from experts 1(screen_understanding, primary visual backbone) → 0(action_planner, click targeting) → 14(input_method, coordinate precision) → 12(memory_context, spatial element recall). Screen understanding is primary because visual grounding IS spatial vision comprehension with coordinate output.

_donor_rationale.expert_1_screen_understanding: Primary donor — visual grounding fundamentally requires screen comprehension (UI layouts, element detection, visual state)
_donor_rationale.expert_0_action_planner: Provides action-to-coordinate mapping (what needs to be clicked and where)
_donor_rationale.expert_14_input_method: Provides coordinate targeting precision (mouse positioning, DPI awareness)
_donor_rationale.expert_12_memory_context: Provides spatial memory (remembering where elements were across screenshots)

expert_17.name: workflow_orchestrator
expert_17.domain: Cross-application workflow execution and multi-app coordination
expert_17.description: Orchestrates complex workflows spanning multiple applications. Handles data transfer between apps (copy from Chrome → paste in Excel), manages window switching sequences, coordinates parallel app operations, handles clipboard state across applications, and ensures data integrity during cross-app transfers. Critical for real-world productivity tasks that span multiple programs.
expert_17.init_strategy: V3: Blend from experts 13(planning_strategy, task decomposition) → 0(action_planner, multi-step sequencing) → 15(app_specific, app interaction patterns) → 5(file_system, data transfer/clipboard). Planning is primary because workflow orchestration IS multi-step planning across applications.

_donor_rationale.expert_13_planning_strategy: Primary donor — workflow orchestration is fundamentally task planning with dependencies and ordering
_donor_rationale.expert_0_action_planner: Provides multi-step action sequence planning within each workflow phase
_donor_rationale.expert_15_app_specific: Provides application-specific knowledge (how Chrome/Excel/VS Code work internally)
_donor_rationale.expert_5_file_system: Provides data transfer capabilities (file operations, clipboard, cross-app data flow)

expert_18.name: verification_oracle
expert_18.domain: Action verification, result validation, and quality assurance
expert_18.description: After every action, verifies that the expected state change occurred. Compares before/after screenshots, validates file contents match expectations, checks that form submissions succeeded, confirms downloads completed, and detects subtle failure modes (silent errors, partial completions, wrong-tab actions). This expert prevents the #1 failure mode of computer agents: claiming success without actual verification.
expert_18.init_strategy: V3: Blend from experts 1(screen_understanding, visual state comparison) → 2(error_recovery, error detection) → 7(safety_ethics, validation/checking) → 12(memory_context, before/after state tracking). Screen understanding and error recovery are co-primary because verification requires SEEING the screen AND understanding what went wrong.

_donor_rationale.expert_1_screen_understanding: Co-primary — verification requires seeing and interpreting the current screen state
_donor_rationale.expert_2_error_recovery: Co-primary — verification must detect errors, failures, and unexpected states
_donor_rationale.expert_7_safety_ethics: Provides validation logic (checking, confirming, ensuring correctness)
_donor_rationale.expert_12_memory_context: Provides before/after comparison capability (remembering what screen looked like before action)

expert_19.name: adaptive_retry
expert_19.domain: Intelligent failure recovery with alternative strategy generation
expert_19.description: When an action fails or the environment is unexpected, generates alternative approaches rather than repeating the same failed action. Maintains a failure history, understands why previous attempts failed, and proposes genuinely different strategies. Handles edge cases: unexpected popups, changed UI layouts, permission escalation needs, network interruptions, and application crashes. This expert is what makes osen-1.0 robust in real-world unpredictable environments.
expert_19.init_strategy: V3: Blend from experts 2(error_recovery, failure diagnosis) → 7(safety_ethics, safe fallback validation) → 3(web_navigation, adaptive navigation/popup handling) → 15(app_specific, app-specific workarounds). Error recovery is primary because adaptive retry IS intelligent error recovery with strategy mutation.

_donor_rationale.expert_2_error_recovery: Primary donor — adaptive retry is fundamentally about recovering from errors with new approaches
_donor_rationale.expert_7_safety_ethics: Provides safe fallback validation (ensuring retry strategies don't cause harm)
_donor_rationale.expert_3_web_navigation: Provides adaptive navigation logic (handling popups, redirects, unexpected states)
_donor_rationale.expert_15_app_specific: Provides application-specific workaround knowledge (app-specific bypass strategies)

expert_co_activation_matrix._comment: V3 NEW: Defines which experts commonly need to work together. Used for training data generation to create cross-expert scenarios.

visual_grounding_verification.scenario: Grounding identifies target → action executed → verification checks result

workflow_verification.scenario: Orchestrator runs workflow step → verification confirms success before next step

grounding_retry.scenario: Grounding fails to find element → adaptive retry tries alternative localization

workflow_retry.scenario: Workflow step fails → adaptive retry generates alternative workflow path

full_chain.scenario: Complete task: ground UI → orchestrate workflow → verify each step → retry on failure

expert_routing_config._comment: V3: Added temperature annealing (soft→sharp routing over training), contrastive/diversity losses to prevent expert collapse.
expert_routing_config._routing_bias_note: The routing_bias values in experts 0-15 are documentary. Only experts 16-19 biases are applied during initialization.

phase_1_expert_warmup.description: Freeze original 16 experts, train only new experts 16-19 on domain-specific data. V3: Increased from 2 to 3 epochs for better specialization convergence.

phase_2_router_calibration.description: V3: Increased from 1 to 2 epochs. Freeze all experts, train only the router to learn proper dispatch to all 20 experts. Uses curriculum learning: specialization→balanced→refinement.

phase_3_full_finetune.description: Fine-tune all experts and router end-to-end on the full osen-1.0 dataset

phase_4_validation_hardening.description: V3 NEW: Final hardening phase with hard examples only (failures, edge cases, adversarial). Low LR to polish without catastrophic forgetting.

dataset_targets._comment: V3: Minimum dataset sizes per expert for quality training. Previous 500/expert was insufficient.

cross_expert_scenarios.description: V3 NEW: Scenarios that exercise multiple new experts in sequence (grounding→verify, workflow→retry, etc.)
